commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair job is appropriate because the code finding from the earlier review is addressed and the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer live behavior proof.; Cleared: The diff only changes Codex app-server transcript projection logic and tests; I found no new dependency, workflow, credential, or supply-chain surface. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Codex app-server resume freshness check that projects newer non-Codex-mirrored OpenClaw history into resumed threads and adds regression tests for the initial and consecutive-resume paths.

PR surface: Source +46, Tests +99. Total +145 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main skips OpenClaw history projection after an existing Codex app-server binding unless another trigger fires, matching the linked Telegram context-loss report; I did not run a live repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair job is appropriate because the code finding from the earlier review is addressed and the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer live behavior proof.

target.title: fix(codex): project newer history on app-server resume
target.body:
## Summary
- project newer external OpenClaw chat history into a resumed Codex app-server thread when the saved binding is older than user-visible transcript messages
- ignore Codex-owned mirrored transcript records during that freshness check so consecutive app-server resumes do not reproject the previous Codex turn back into Codex
- add regression coverage for both the original resumed-binding projection path and the repeated-resume mirror-filter path

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: resumed Codex app-server turns must receive newer external OpenClaw transcript context once, but a later resume must not treat Codex's own mirrored prompt/assistant records as new external context and reproject them.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw Codex app-server extension runtime tests on commit `a025e35f12`, using real session JSONL files, persisted Codex app-server binding files, and the app-server `thread/resume` plus `turn/start` request flow. I also attempted a live local Codex app-server capture with the real client factory, but the local app-server startup timed out before `turn/start`, so the proof below is the deterministic source-level resumed-binding path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "consecutive resumes|projects newer mirrored history"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix:
- The original regression path writes an existing Codex thread binding, appends newer external OpenClaw user and assistant transcript messages, resumes the Codex app-server thread, and verifies the emitted `turn/start` input contains `OpenClaw assembled context for this turn:`, the prior Sonnet-leak context, the prior David Ondrej assistant context, and the current request.
- The new repeated-resume regression first verifies that newer external context is projected on resume 1. It then appends Codex-owned mirrored prompt/assistant records with newer timestamps and performs resume 2. The emitted `turn/start` input for resume 2 does not contain `OpenClaw assembled context for this turn:`, does not contain the earlier external Sonnet/David Ondrej context, does not contain the first resume prompt, and does contain only the current resume prompt.
- Focused run result: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 2 passed | 222 skipped (224)`.
- Full relevant run result: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 237 passed (237)`.
- The two CI-failing lint shards were reproduced locally and passed after the one-line bracket-access fix: `pnpm lint --threads=8` and `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`.

Observed result after fix: existing-binding resume still uses `thread/resume` and projects newer external OpenClaw context into Codex input, while consecutive resumes ignore Codex app-server mirror records identified by `codex-app-server:` idempotency keys or mirror metadata.

What was not tested: a live Telegram DM or a live Codex OAuth turn; local live app-server startup timed out before a `turn/start` capture. The source-level proof exercises the resumed Codex app-server path identified by ClawSweeper.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "consecutive resumes|projects newer mirrored history"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `git diff --check`

Fixes #86449

Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>
